21.correct: The 97th Constitutional Amendment (2011) gave constitutional status to cooperative societies, adding the right to form them and Part IX-B.

wrong.A: The 73rd Amendment dealt with Panchayati Raj.
wrong.B: The 86th Amendment made education a fundamental right.
wrong.C: The 91st Amendment limited the size of the Council of Ministers.

21.fact: The 97th Amendment added 'cooperative societies' to the right to form associations (Article 19).

22.correct: The Dhol is the percussion instrument commonly used during a Giddha performance.

wrong.A: The tabla is used in classical/semi-classical music.
wrong.C: The harmonium is not the main Giddha instrument.
wrong.D: The sarangi is a classical bowed instrument.

22.fact: Giddha is the women's folk dance of Punjab, danced to boliyan and the beat of the dhol.

23.correct: The Northern Plains are famous for rich alluvial soil and a high population density.

wrong.A: The peninsular plateau has older, less fertile soils.
wrong.C: Coastal plains are fertile but smaller.
wrong.D: The Himalayas have thin mountain soils and low density.

23.fact: The fertile, well-watered Northern Plains support dense populations and intensive farming.

24.correct: J.J. Thomson discovered the electron in 1897 through his cathode-ray-tube experiments.

wrong.A: Ernest Rutherford discovered the nucleus and proton.
wrong.C: Niels Bohr gave the model of the atom.

24.fact: Thomson's discovery showed that atoms contain smaller, negatively charged particles.

25.correct: The Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya (formerly Prince of Wales Museum), Mumbai, is a major example of Indo-Gothic/Indo-Saracenic colonial architecture in India.

wrong.B: The Taj Mahal is Mughal architecture.
wrong.D: The Red Fort is Mughal architecture.

25.fact: Indo-Saracenic style blended Gothic, Indian and Islamic features in colonial-era public buildings.

26.correct: In kho-kho, 3 defenders enter the field at the start of a defending turn (innings).

wrong.A: 2 is fewer than the number that enter.

26.fact: Defenders enter in batches of three and try to avoid being tagged by the chasers.

27.correct: Maize (Zea mays) is a crop used both as food (for humans) and as fodder (for animals).

wrong.A: Barley is mainly a food/feed grain but maize is the classic dual food-and-fodder crop here.
wrong.B: Sugarcane is grown mainly for sugar.
wrong.D: Sorghum (jowar) is used as food/fodder too, but maize is the keyed answer.

27.fact: Maize is one of the world's most important cereal crops, also used for starch and biofuel.

28.correct: The Gurjara-Pratihara ruler Harichandra is known for establishing the dynasty's kingship in the Rajasthan region (II), so 'Only II' is correct.

wrong.A: 'Only I' (Tamil Nadu) is wrong; the Pratiharas rose in the north-west.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since Tamil Nadu is not associated with him.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since Rajasthan is correct.

28.fact: The Gurjara-Pratiharas were a major power in northern India and resisted Arab expansion.

29.correct: In April 2025, Alok Joshi was appointed the head of the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB).

wrong.A: Ajit Doval is the National Security Adviser (NSA), a different post.
wrong.C: Shivshankar Menon is a former NSA.
wrong.D: Brajesh Mishra was India's first NSA.

29.fact: The NSAB advises the National Security Council on long-term security issues.

30.correct: Prem Singh Tamang (P.S. Golay) took oath for a second term as Chief Minister of Sikkim in 2024.

wrong.B: He is not the CM of Mizoram.
wrong.C: He is not the CM of Arunachal Pradesh.
wrong.D: He is not the CM of Meghalaya.

30.fact: His party, the SKM, won a landslide in the 2024 Sikkim Assembly elections.

31.correct: In India, One Nation One Subscription provides access to more than 13,000 scholarly journals from about 30 major international publishers.

wrong.D: 30,000 refers loosely to scale but the figure is about 13,000 journals.

31.fact: ONOS gives government higher-education and research institutions nationwide access to research journals.

32.correct: There is no category called an 'urgent question' in the Indian Parliament, so it is the one not asked.

wrong.A: A starred question seeks an oral answer.
wrong.B: An unstarred question seeks a written answer.
wrong.D: A short-notice question is a recognised category.

32.fact: Parliamentary questions are mainly starred, unstarred, short-notice, and questions to private members.

33.correct: India added 683 new animal species to its faunal records in 2024.

wrong.A: 786 is not the figure for new fauna species.

33.fact: The Zoological Survey of India documents the addition of new faunal species each year.

34.correct: The Gaganyaan mission is planned as a 3-day human spaceflight, launching a crew into orbit and bringing them safely back.

wrong.B: 5 days is not the planned duration.

34.fact: Gaganyaan will be India's first crewed orbital spaceflight mission.

35.correct: The Combat Air Teaming System (CATS) Warrior, an unmanned aerial vehicle concept, has been developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).

wrong.B: DRDO develops various systems but CATS Warrior is led by HAL.
wrong.C: ISRO is the space agency, not the developer.

35.fact: The CATS Warrior is a 'loyal wingman' drone designed to operate alongside fighter jets.

36.correct: New Delhi hosted the World Para Athletics Championships in 2025.
36.fact: It was the first time India hosted the World Para Athletics Championships.

37.correct: The UN Secretary-General is appointed for a term of 5 years (renewable).

wrong.D: 6 years is not the standard term.

37.fact: The Secretary-General is appointed by the General Assembly on the Security Council's recommendation.

38.correct: The wind instrument 'Kali', used in Assam, is regarded as a more developed version of the shehnai.

wrong.A: The jhanj is a pair of cymbals, not a wind instrument.
wrong.C: A drum is a percussion instrument.
wrong.D: A guitar is a string instrument.

38.fact: Wind instruments of the shehnai family are common in Indian folk and ceremonial music.

39.correct: The Swachh Survekshan survey design is based on 3 main pillars (broad components).

wrong.B: 5 is not the number of pillars.

39.fact: Swachh Survekshan is the annual cleanliness survey of cities under the Swachh Bharat Mission.

40.correct: According to the SIPRI report (March 2025), Ukraine was the world's largest arms importer in 2020-24, driven by the ongoing war.

wrong.B: Brazil was not the largest importer.
wrong.C: Japan was not the largest importer.
wrong.D: North Korea was not the largest importer.

40.fact: SIPRI tracks global arms transfers; large imports often reflect active conflicts or modernisation drives.
